title: Contribution Margin Analysis: A company incurs a variable cost of 40 per unit...
summary: Calculate the units required to achieve a target profit using the contribution margin approach.
prompt: A company incurs a variable cost of $40 per unit and sells the product for $100. If the fixed costs are $200,000, how many units must be sold to achieve a profit of $50,000?
solution: Units to be sold = (Fixed Costs + Desired Profit) / Contribution Margin per Unit = ($200,000 + $50,000) / ($100 - $40) = 6,250 units. This calculation is crucial for financial planning (Page 47).
